vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are not visible or have suffered total systemic loss.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in black pot; white rabbit anchor is present but isolated.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of succulent growth.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of mint foliage.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil appears devoid of Pothos.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no surface cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of a white cylindrical object (likely a supplement or tool) in the vicinity of the yellow pot; white debris/fragments on the soil surface of the black pot containing the rabbit anchor.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static, non-vegetated state. No growth, senescence, or movement of plant matter has occurred over the 5-day period.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total biological failure. The pots contain only substrate and non-living debris. No photosynthetic activity is occurring.
anomalies: White granular debris on soil surface (Potential mineral buildup or decorative intervention)
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish the spatial orientation of the pots and the scale anchor. Upon validation, it is clear that the expected botanical specimens (P1-P4) are absent. The environment is currently a 'dead' biome consisting only of soil, the white rabbit anchor, and a foreign cylindrical object. There is no evidence of plant life in any of the images provided.
